premise: I took in my nephew at eight when my sister died, and at 52 I used $50,000 of my nursing pension to put him through culinary school and launch a food truck with a handshake 50/50 deal; once the truck became a citywide sensation, he secretly trademarked the name and fired me, claiming I was just a volunteer. I sued for breach of partnership, won full ownership of the original truck and the trademark, and now park my new food truck across the street every day serving a ‘Traitor’s Wrap’ special — his copycat business folded within six months, and I bought his last fryer at the liquidation sale for $200.

thumb.label: Thumbnail + metadata
thumb.detail: My Nephew Stole My Food Truck And Called Me A Volunteer. So I Parked Across The
